title: James Perry
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: James Perry
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: I received my BS('03), MA('08) and PhD('13) in Mathematics from Binghamton University. During my last year of my PhD program I was an adjuct lecturer. After completing my PhD, I was a lecturer at Binghamton University for the 2013/2014 school year. In the Fall of 2014, I became a member of the CNU Math department.
education_entries: Ph D in Mathematics, Binghamton University
research_interests: I am interested in finite group theory and lattice theory. In particular, I am interested in how lattice theoretic properties in the subgroup lattice of a group control the structure of the group.
teaching_interests: While I enjoy teaching all math classes, I particularly enjoy teaching calculus, discrete math, linear algebra and modern algebra.
